name: timezone
description: This function has two distinct behaviors based on the arguments provided. When given a timezone string and a timestamp, it converts between timezone-naive (`TIMESTAMP`) and timezone-aware (`TIMESTAMPTZ`) types. It can convert a naive timestamp into an aware one by interpreting it in the specified timezone, or convert an aware timestamp into a naive one representing the local time in that zone. When given a single temporal argument (date, timestamp, or interval), it functions as a date part extraction function, returning the timezone offset in seconds.
short_description: Converts timestamps between timezones or extracts the timezone offset in seconds.

parameters.name: zone
parameters.description: The time zone to use for the conversion (e.g., 'America/Denver', 'UTC').

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: The timezone-naive timestamp to convert.

parameters.name: zone
parameters.description: The time zone to use for the conversion (e.g., 'America/Denver', 'UTC').

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: The timezone-aware timestamp to convert.

parameters.name: temporal
parameters.description: The temporal value from which to extract the timezone offset in seconds.

name: timezone_hour
description: Extracts the hour component of the time zone offset from a date, timestamp, or interval. Note that the time zone parts are all zero unless a time zone extension such as ICU has been installed to support TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE.
short_description: Extracts the hour portion of the time zone offset from a temporal value.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The temporal value from which to extract the timezone hour portion.

name: timezone_minute
description: Extracts the minute component of the time zone offset from a temporal value (DATE, TIMESTAMP, TIMESTAMPTZ, or INTERVAL). Note that the time zone parts are all zero unless a time zone extension such as ICU has been installed to support TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE.
short_description: Extracts the minute portion of the time zone offset from a temporal value.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The temporal value from which to extract the time zone minute.

name: to_base64
description: Converts a blob to a base64 encoded string.
short_description: Converts a blob to a base64 encoded string.

parameters.name: blob
parameters.description: The blob to be converted.

name: to_base
description: Converts a number to a string representation in a specified base (radix). The output can be optionally padded with leading zeros to meet a minimum length.
short_description: Converts a number to a string in a given base, with optional zero-padding.

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: The number to be converted.

parameters.name: radix
parameters.description: The base to convert the number to.

parameters.name: min_length
parameters.description: The minimum length of the resulting string, padded with leading zeros if necessary.

name: to_binary
description: Converts a string or an integer value to its binary string representation.
short_description: Converts a string or integer to its binary representation.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The string or integer value to be converted to a binary string.

name: to_centuries
description: Constructs an INTERVAL from an integer value representing a number of centuries. One century is equivalent to 100 years.
short_description: Constructs an interval of a specified number of centuries.

parameters.name: integer
parameters.description: The number of centuries.

name: to_days
description: Constructs an interval representing a specified number of days. The function takes an integer value as input and returns an INTERVAL data type.
short_description: Constructs a day interval from an integer.

parameters.name: integer
parameters.description: The number of days.

name: to_decades
description: Constructs an interval from a given number of decades. Each decade is equivalent to 10 years.
short_description: Constructs an interval of a specified number of decades.

parameters.name: integer
parameters.description: The number of decades.

name: to_hours
description: Constructs an interval representing a specified number of hours.
short_description: Constructs an interval of a specified number of hours.

parameters.name: integer
parameters.description: The number of hours.

name: to_json
description: Converts any DuckDB value into a JSON string representation. Lists are converted to JSON arrays, while structs and maps are converted to JSON objects.
short_description: Converts any value into a JSON string

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value to be converted to JSON. Can be of any data type. Lists are converted to JSON arrays, and structs/maps are converted to JSON objects.

name: to_microseconds
description: Constructs an interval from a given number of microseconds. This function takes an integer value and returns an INTERVAL data type representing that many microseconds.
short_description: Constructs an interval of a specified number of microseconds.

parameters.name: integer
parameters.description: The number of microseconds.

name: to_millennia
description: Constructs an interval representing a specified number of millennia. One millennium is equivalent to 1000 years.
short_description: Constructs an interval of a specified number of millennia.

parameters.name: integer
parameters.description: The number of millennia.

name: to_milliseconds
description: Constructs an INTERVAL value from a given number of milliseconds. The input can be an integer or a floating-point number.
short_description: Construct a millisecond interval from a number.

parameters.name: milliseconds
parameters.description: The number of milliseconds.

name: to_minutes
description: Constructs an interval representing a specified number of minutes. This function takes an integer value and converts it into an INTERVAL data type.
short_description: Constructs an interval of a specified number of minutes.

parameters.name: integer
parameters.description: The number of minutes for the interval.

name: to_months
description: Constructs an INTERVAL value from an integer representing a number of months. This is useful for date and time arithmetic where you need to add or subtract a specific number of months.
short_description: Constructs an interval of a specified number of months.

parameters.name: integer
parameters.description: The number of months for the interval.

name: to_quarters
description: Constructs an interval from a given number of quarters. Each quarter is equivalent to 3 months.
short_description: Constructs an interval from a given number of quarters.
usage_example: SELECT to_quarters(5); -- Returns INTERVAL 1 YEAR 3 MONTHS

parameters.name: integer
parameters.description: The number of quarters.

name: to_seconds
description: Constructs an INTERVAL value from a given number of seconds. The input can be a fractional value.
short_description: Constructs an interval of a specified number of seconds.

parameters.name: seconds
parameters.description: The number of seconds for the interval.

name: to_timestamp
description: Converts a numeric value representing the number of seconds since the Unix epoch (1970-01-01 00:00:00+00) into a timestamp with a time zone.
short_description: Converts seconds since the epoch to a timestamp with time zone.

parameters.name: sec
parameters.description: The number of seconds since the epoch (1970-01-01 00:00:00+00).

name: to_weeks
description: Constructs an interval from a given number of weeks. The function takes an integer representing the number of weeks and returns the corresponding INTERVAL value. One week is treated as equivalent to 7 days. For example, `to_weeks(5)` results in an interval of 35 days.
short_description: Constructs an interval of a specified number of weeks.

parameters.name: integer
parameters.description: The number of weeks to be converted into an interval.

name: to_years
description: Constructs an interval representing a specified number of years from an integer value.
short_description: Constructs a year interval from an integer.

parameters.name: integer
parameters.description: The number of years.

name: today
description: Returns the current date in the local time zone at the start of the current transaction.
short_description: Returns the current date at the start of the transaction in the local time zone.

name: tpcds
description: Runs a specific TPC-DS benchmark query identified by its ID. The TPC-DS data must be generated first using the `dsdgen` function. Note that this pragma runs a fixed query with pre-defined bind parameters, which cannot be changed.
short_description: Runs a specific TPC-DS benchmark query by its ID.

parameters.name: query_id
parameters.description: The identifier of the TPC-DS query to execute.

name: tpch
description: Runs a specific TPC-H benchmark query. The TPC-H extension provides the data generator and queries for the TPC-H benchmark. This pragma executes one of the 22 standard TPC-H queries, identified by its number.
short_description: Runs a specific TPC-H benchmark query by its number.

parameters.name: query_number
parameters.description: The TPC-H query number to execute.

name: tpch_answers
description: Returns the expected results for all 22 TPC-H queries for the specific scale factors of 0.01, 0.1, and 1. This function is part of the TPC-H extension and is useful for verifying the correctness of query results against a known baseline.
short_description: Returns the expected results for TPC-H queries for scale factors 0.01, 0.1, and 1.

name: tpch_queries
description: Lists all 22 TPC-H benchmark queries. This function returns a table containing the query number and the full SQL text for each of the 22 standard TPC-H queries.
short_description: Lists all 22 TPC-H benchmark queries and their SQL text.

name: translate
description: Replaces each character in the input string that matches a character in the 'from' set with the corresponding character in the 'to' set. If the 'from' set is longer than the 'to' set, occurrences of the extra characters in 'from' are deleted from the result.
short_description: Replaces characters in a string based on a mapping of 'from' and 'to' character sets.

parameters.name: string
parameters.description: The input string to be translated.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: A string containing the set of characters to be replaced.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: A string containing the set of characters to replace with. Each character corresponds by position to a character in the 'from' string.

name: trim
description: Removes any occurrences of the specified characters from both the beginning and end of a string. If no characters are specified, it removes leading and trailing whitespace.
short_description: Removes specified characters or whitespace from both sides of a string

parameters.name: string
parameters.description: The string to trim.

parameters.name: characters
parameters.description: The set of characters to remove from the string. Defaults to whitespace.

name: trunc
description: Truncates a number by removing its fractional part, effectively rounding towards zero. An optional second argument specifies the number of decimal places to truncate to.
short_description: Truncates a number to an integer or a specified number of decimal places.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: The numeric value to truncate.

parameters.name: decimal_places
parameters.description: The number of decimal places to truncate to. If not provided, the number is truncated to an integer.

name: try_bind
description: Validates a SQL query's syntax and verifies that all referenced tables and columns exist, without executing the query. This is particularly useful for checking AI-generated queries before presenting them to a user. The function returns a single row with two columns: `error_message` and `error_type`. If the query is valid, `error_type` will be 'ok' and `error_message` will be empty. If the query is invalid, `error_type` will indicate the failure type (e.g., 'parser', 'binder') and `error_message` will contain the details.
short_description: Validates a SQL query's syntax and checks for the existence of tables/columns without executing it.
usage_example: CALL try_bind('SELECT * FORM orders'); -- Returns an error_type (e.g., 'parser') and a descriptive error_message

parameters.name: sql_query
parameters.description: The SQL query string to validate.

name: try_strptime
description: Converts a string to a timestamp according to a specified format. This function is similar to strptime, but instead of throwing an error on a parsing failure, it returns NULL. It can accept a single format string or a list of format strings, which it will try in order until one succeeds.
short_description: Converts a string to a timestamp using a format string, returning NULL on failure.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: The input string to convert to a timestamp.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The format string or a list of format strings to use for parsing the input string. If a list is provided, they are tried in order until one succeeds.

name: txid_current
description: Returns the current transaction's identifier. It will assign a new one if the current transaction does not have one already.
short_description: Returns the current transaction's identifier

name: typeof
description: Returns the name of the data type of the result of the expression.
short_description: Returns the name of the data type of an expression

parameters.name: expression
parameters.description: The expression for which to determine the data type.

name: unicode
description: Returns an integer representing the Unicode codepoint of the first character in the string. If the string is empty, it returns -1.
short_description: Returns the Unicode codepoint of the first character in a string.

parameters.name: string
parameters.description: The input string from which to extract the Unicode codepoint of the first character.

name: union_extract
description: Extracts the value associated with a specific tag from a UNION type. If the specified tag is not the currently active tag in the union instance, it returns NULL. This function can also be called using dot notation (e.g., my_union.my_tag), which serves as an alias for this function.
short_description: Extracts a value from a UNION type by its tag name

parameters.name: union
parameters.description: The UNION object from which to extract the value.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: The name of the tag whose value should be extracted.

name: union_tag
description: A UNION type is a tagged union, always carrying a discriminator 'tag' which signals which alternative value it is currently holding. This function retrieves that tag as an ENUM type.
short_description: Retrieves the currently selected tag of a UNION as an ENUM.

parameters.name: union
parameters.description: The UNION value from which to extract the tag.

name: union_value
description: Creates a single-member UNION containing the argument value. The tag of the value will be the bound variable name (i.e., the name of the parameter provided).
short_description: Creates a single-member UNION with a specified tag and value.

parameters.name: member
parameters.description: A named argument where the parameter's name is used as the tag and its value is the single member of the resulting UNION.

name: unnest
description: Unnests a list or struct by one level, expanding its elements into multiple rows (for lists) or columns (for structs). It can be used in the `SELECT` clause. When multiple lists are unnested in the same query, they are processed side-by-side, with shorter lists being padded with NULL values. Recursive unnesting can be enabled to flatten nested structures, with an optional depth limit.
short_description: Unnests a list or struct, expanding its elements into multiple rows or columns.

parameters.name: input_collection
parameters.description: The list or struct to unnest.

parameters.name: recursive
parameters.description: If true, unnests lists and structs recursively.

parameters.name: max_depth
parameters.description: Limits the depth of recursive unnesting. Using this parameter implies recursive unnesting.

name: unpivot_list
description: Identical to list_value, but generated as part of unpivot for better error messages.
short_description: Identical to list_value; used internally by UNPIVOT for better error messages.

parameters.name: arg
parameters.description: An argument to be included in the list.
Additional arguments to be included in the list.

name: upper
description: Converts a string to upper case.
short_description: Converts a string to upper case.

parameters.name: string
parameters.description: The string to convert to uppercase.

name: url_decode
description: Decodes a URL from a representation using Percent-Encoding. This process converts percent-encoded characters (e.g., '%20') back to their original representation (e.g., a space).
short_description: Decodes a URL-encoded string using Percent-Encoding

parameters.name: string
parameters.description: The URL-encoded string to be decoded.

name: url_encode
description: Encodes a string into a URL-safe format using Percent-Encoding. This process replaces special characters (like spaces, slashes, and plus signs) with their corresponding percent-encoded representation (e.g., '%20', '%2F', '%2B'). This is particularly useful for handling slashes in column names when writing to partitioned datasets.
short_description: Encodes a string for use in a URL using Percent-Encoding
usage_example: SELECT url_encode('this string has/ special+ characters>');

parameters.name: string
parameters.description: The string to be URL-encoded.

name: user
description: Returns the user name of the current session.
short_description: Returns the current session's user name.

name: user_agent
description: Returns the user agent information for the current DuckDB instance. The user agent string typically includes the DuckDB version and the platform it's running on, for example, 'duckdb/v0.10.0(osx_arm64)'.
short_description: Returns the user agent information for the DuckDB instance.

name: uuid
description: Returns a random Version 4 Universally Unique Identifier (UUIDv4). The result is a 128-bit number represented as a string in the standard format, for example: 'eeccb8c5-9943-b2bb-bb5e-222f4e14b687'.
short_description: Generates a random Version 4 Universally Unique Identifier (UUID).

name: uuid_extract_timestamp
description: Extracts the timestamp component from a version 7 UUID (UUIDv7) value.
short_description: Extracts the timestamp from a UUIDv7 value

parameters.name: uuidv7
parameters.description: The UUIDv7 value from which to extract the timestamp.

name: uuid_extract_version
description: Extracts the version number from a given UUID. The returned version will typically be 4 or 7, corresponding to the UUID version.
short_description: Extracts the version number (4 or 7) from a UUID.

parameters.name: uuid
parameters.description: The UUID value from which to extract the version number.

name: uuidv7
description: Generates a random Version 7 Universally Unique Identifier (UUIDv7). Unlike UUIDv4, UUIDv7 values are time-ordered and contain a timestamp component, making them suitable for use as primary keys in distributed systems.
short_description: Generates a random, time-ordered UUIDv7.

name: var_pop
description: Calculates the population variance, which does not include bias correction. The formula used is `(sum(x^2) - sum(x)^2 / count(x)) / count(x)`.
short_description: Calculates the population variance, which does not include bias correction.
usage_example: SELECT var_pop(c) FROM VALUES (1), (2), (3), (4) AS t(c);

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: The set of values to calculate the population variance for.

name: variance
description: Calculates the sample variance of a set of numbers. This function uses Bessel's correction, which adjusts the result to be an unbiased estimator of the population variance. The formula used is `(sum(x^2) - sum(x)^2 / count(x)) / (count(x) - 1)`. NULL values are ignored in the calculation.
short_description: Calculates the sample variance, which includes Bessel's bias correction.

parameters.name: expression
parameters.description: The expression over which to calculate the sample variance.

name: verify_external
description: Enables verification of external operators. This is a development and testing feature.
short_description: Enables verification of external operators

name: verify_parallelism
description: Forces queries to be executed in parallel. This pragma is primarily used for development and internal testing to ensure that the parallel execution paths of the query engine are correctly exercised.
short_description: Forces queries to be executed in parallel for testing purposes.

name: verify_serializer
description: Enables verification of round-trip capabilities for supported logical plans. This is primarily used for development and internal testing.
short_description: Enables verification of round-trip capabilities for logical plans.

name: version
description: Shows the version of the running DuckDB instance. This pragma returns a table with a single column, `library_version`, which contains the version string. It can be invoked using `PRAGMA version;` or `CALL pragma_version();`.
short_description: Shows the version of the running DuckDB instance.

name: version
description: Returns the currently active version of DuckDB as a string. The format is typically 'v' followed by the version number, for example, 'v0.3.2'.
short_description: Returns the currently active version of DuckDB

name: week
description: Extracts the ISO week number from a date, timestamp, or interval. This function is part of a set of functions for manipulating temporal data types.
short_description: Extracts the ISO week number from a date, timestamp, or interval.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The date, timestamp, or interval from which to extract the ISO week number.

name: weighted_avg
description: Calculates the weighted average of all non-null values in a column. Each value is scaled by its corresponding weight. If a weight is NULL, the corresponding value is also skipped.
short_description: Calculates the weighted average of a set of values, where each value is scaled by a corresponding weight.

parameters.name: arg
parameters.description: The column containing the values to be averaged.

parameters.name: weight
parameters.description: The column containing the corresponding weights for each value.

name: which_secret
description: The `which_secret` table function is used to determine which secret will be used for a given file path and secret type. When multiple secrets of the same type exist (e.g., for different S3 buckets), this function helps identify the specific secret chosen based on scope matching rules. The secret with the longest matching scope prefix for the given path is selected.
short_description: Shows which secret is used for a given path and secret type.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: The file path for which to check the secret.

parameters.name: secret_type
parameters.description: The type of the secret to check (e.g., 's3', 'gcs', 'azure').

name: write_log
description: Writes a custom message to the DuckDB logger. This function allows for detailed logging by specifying the message content, as well as optional parameters for the logging level, scope, and a custom log type. The logged messages can be inspected using the duckdb_logs() table function.
short_description: Writes a custom message to the DuckDB logger.
usage_example: SELECT write_log('A new duck has connected to the lake.', level := 'debug', scope := 'connection', log_type := 'duckdb.docs.example.quack');

parameters.name: message
parameters.description: The log message to write.

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: The logging level for the message (e.g., 'debug', 'info').

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: The scope of the log message (e.g., 'connection').

parameters.name: log_type
parameters.description: A custom type for the log message.

name: xor
description: Performs a bitwise XOR (exclusive OR) operation on two inputs. This function works with both integer types and bitstrings. When used with bitstrings, both inputs must be of the same length.
short_description: Performs a bitwise XOR operation on two integers or bitstrings.
usage_example: SELECT xor(17, 5) AS int_xor, xor('101'::BITSTRING, '001'::BITSTRING) AS bit_xor;

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: The first input value, which can be an integer or a bitstring.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: The second input value, which can be an integer or a bitstring.

name: year
description: Extracts the Gregorian year as an integer from a temporal value. This function is a dedicated extraction function for the 'year' date part.
short_description: Extracts the year from a date, timestamp, or interval.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The temporal value (date, timestamp, or interval) from which to extract the year.

name: yearweek
description: Extracts the ISO year and week number from a date, timestamp, or interval, returning it as a single integer in YYYYWW format.
short_description: Returns the ISO year and week number as a single integer (YYYYWW)

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The date, timestamp, or interval from which to extract the year and week.
